_comment: ADOPTER EXAMPLE — Meridian Trust (DEMO, synthetic, non-production). A worked operations-signal log showing the Run -> Discovery loop close: every production signal is triaged (routed) and traceable, so nothing falls on the floor. Verified by scripts/operations-signal-check.mjs. See README.md. Replace with your own signals; keep the shape.

signals.summary: Consent-revoke acknowledgement lagged 9s p95 in prod after a deploy; within SLA but user-visible. The build is wrong, the problem is fine.
Newly-disclosed CVE in a shipped TLS dependency raised the residual position of the personal-data handling risk.
Recurring confusion about what 'revoke' actually cancels — the framing may be wrong, not the code. Being scoped into a discovery run; the loop reopens.
Consent API error budget burned 18% in a transient spike; recovered, still within the quarterly budget.
signals.justification: Within the error budget; no line change warranted this window. Re-assess if the next window also spikes.

signals.summary: Standards errata tightened consent-record retention wording; touches the personal-data category. The risk position moved.
Classifier confidence drifted on the revoke-reason field; a bounded prompt/patch restores it without touching the problem.
A previously-inherent access-scope risk materialised in a near-miss; residual position updated in the register with a compensating control.
